rationale: The thread closed this as a duplicate of #4808 (not a merged fix), but X confidently asserts the bug is 'already fixed on main' with fabricated line-by-line quotes of a specific comment and a claim that #4808 was 'closed, superseded' by #4810 — this inverts the actual relationship (a duplicate points to a still-relevant target, not a dead one) and risks telling the reporter a false all-clear. Y reaches the same duplicate-closure conclusion but is honest about unverifiable claims, explicitly flags that #4808 shows CLOSED/mergedAt-null and asks for confirmation rather than asserting resolution, and adds a concrete, grounded follow-up (test coverage gap) that engages with the real uncertainty in the thread. Y is better calibrated to the ground truth's terse duplicate-pointer resolution and avoids X's fabricated-evidence risk.
